mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.0% in April 2011 indicates post-crisis labor market recovery phase
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389.4B in April 2011 reflects ongoing consumer spending recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rate (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above 2.5% annually, real rates turn negative, compressing bond yields and driving equity risk premium compression that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPIAUCSL at 224.4 in April 2011 creates negative real rate environment
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL_growth) produces Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher when both conditions are met versus when only one is met (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals strengthening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, indicating robust economic momentum that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.6 in March 2011 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration > 0.4% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.2%, t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market that increases wage pressures and consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both conditions strengthen simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131.0M and UNRATE at 9.0% in April 2011 show labor market stabilization
regime_hint: labor market tightening with employment gains
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS_growth × UNRATE_decline) produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window, or SPX returns when both conditions are met do not exceed returns when only one is met by at least 1.5% on average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 500K units signals weakening residential investment and future consumer wealth destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as forward-looking equity prices discount reduced economic growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549K in March 2011 remains below pre-crisis levels, indicating housing market weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.01 (fails to show negative correlation) over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when HOUST < 500K versus HOUST >= 500K (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 1%, the Fed faces a policy dilemma that creates uncertainty about future rate paths, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13.4T in Q1 2011 shows moderate growth; FEDFUNDS at 0.1% provides policy accommodation
regime_hint: monetary policy uncertainty with slowing growth
failure_condition: The interaction term produces Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year window, or SPX returns when both conditions are met do not show significantly negative bias compared to baseline (mean return > 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity prices discount reduced earnings potential.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.6 and UNRATE at 9.0% in March-April 2011 show stabilization, not deterioration
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market weakness
failure_condition: The joint signal produces Rank IC > -0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day forward returns following both conditions are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.1%, t-test p > 0.20).
